task_completion.description: Verify that the Wazuh configuration interface or file is opened for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Wazuh Dashboard configuration XML editor or a terminal text editor (like nano or vim) editing the ossec.conf file.

task_completion.description: Verify that email notifications are enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the XML tag <email_notification>yes</email_notification> or a corresponding UI toggle set to 'yes' in the configuration.

task_completion.description: Verify that the SMTP server is configured correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the XML tag <smtp_server>smtp.company.internal</smtp_server> in the global configuration section.

task_completion.description: Verify that the destination email address is configured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the XML tag <email_to>security-alerts@company.com</email_to> in the configuration.

task_completion.description: Verify that the sender email address is configured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the XML tag <email_from>wazuh@company.com</email_from> in the configuration.

task_completion.description: Verify that the maximum number of emails per hour is configured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the XML tag <email_maxperhour>12</email_maxperhour> in the configuration.

integrity.description: Verify the agent is interacting with a real Wazuh environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for genuine Wazuh dashboard UI elements (menus, logos) or standard terminal paths like /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just type the answers into an empty text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the surrounding text contains standard Wazuh configuration XML tags (e.g., <ossec_config>, <global>, <alerts>) and is not just an isolated list of the requested parameters.

integrity.description: Verify that the configuration changes were saved.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save' button being clicked, a 'Configuration saved successfully' toast message in the GUI, or terminal commands indicating the file was saved and the wazuh-manager service was restarted.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected Wazuh configuration values are: email_notification = 'yes', smtp_server = 'smtp.company.internal', email_to = 'security-alerts@company.com', email_from = 'wazuh@company.com', and email_maxperhour = 12.
